name: Black Sea Region
climate: Continental to subtropical along southern coast; cold winters in the north, mild Mediterranean-influenced climate in the south

neighbors.name: Turkish Straits
neighbors.relevance: Sole maritime exit from the Black Sea; Montreux Convention controls naval and commercial access

neighbors.name: Mediterranean Sea Basin
neighbors.relevance: Black Sea trade flows enter the Mediterranean via the Turkish Straits

neighbors.name: Commonwealth of Independent States
neighbors.relevance: Russia and Georgia are CIS members; Ukraine was formerly associated; conflict zone

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Romania and Bulgaria are EU members on the Black Sea; EU sanctions affect the region

neighbors.name: Levant
neighbors.relevance: Eastern Mediterranean energy and security dynamics connect to Black Sea geopolitics

neighbors.name: Persian Gulf
neighbors.relevance: Caspian-Black Sea energy pipelines (BTC, TANAP) link Gulf-adjacent producers to Black Sea ports

links.description: All Black Sea maritime trade must transit the Turkish Straits; Turkey controls access under the Montreux Convention
Grain, oil, and steel exports flow from Black Sea ports through the Straits to Mediterranean markets
Russia-Ukraine war has militarized the Black Sea; grain corridor agreements are fragile
Romania and Bulgaria connect Black Sea logistics to EU inland markets via Danube and rail
Ukrainian grain rerouted through Romania and Poland to V4 logistics networks during wartime
Alternative grain export routes connect Black Sea origins to Baltic Sea ports via rail and river
Black Sea grain shipments transit Turkish Straits and then Suez Canal to reach Asian markets
